verdict_reasoning: IZUMO1 is a type-I single-pass transmembrane immunoglobulin-superfamily protein with a ~300-residue extracellular Izumo+Ig domain ectodomain and a short cytoplasmic tail. Expression is strictly restricted to the testis/sperm lineage. In fresh spermatozoa it resides in the inner acrosomal membrane and is not surface-exposed; only after capacitation and the acrosome reaction does it translocate to the outer sperm plasma membrane (equatorial segment) where it engages egg JUNO/FCRL3. Its own TM anchor is unambiguous, but surface display is confined to a narrow germline lineage and acrosome-reacted state, fitting tissue_restricted_surface rather than yes.
